Rosetta Stone Success

I minored in French in college, so I got this mainly to refresh my memory and improve my pronunciation.  I think if I had no background in French, though, I'd be tearing my hair out in frustration.  There's just not enough context for things to make sense.  Part of my problem is I'm not an auditory learner--I learn far more easily by reading than by listening.  While kids might learn by listening, I'm not a kid and trying to learn like a child just isn't going to work for me as an adult.

The software is a solid product but customer support and documentation is minimal.  If you like to know how things work, need help working out technical issues then this product is on thin ice. As long as things go well with no problems - it has all of the basic functionality and is easy to use.  I would think a product of this magnitude would have better support or at least a user manual.

I have used TurboTax for eight years now. I used a few other tax services prior to, but TurboTax is absolutely the best choice. TurboTax imports the prior year's information so you don't have to start from scratch. The process of importing various tax documents from different financial institutions is so easy. My taxes are more complicated than ever this year due to having multiple W2s, student loan interest, paying into two standard 401k and two Roth 401k, cashing out of a Roth IRA, selling stock at a loss, receiving dividends, donations to 4 separate charities, etc. I was dreading my taxes this year but the process was as painless. I rarely pay for the upgrade but I decided to add Max this year to protect against audits and that gives me peace of mind. My only complaint is that it costs $40 if you choose to pay for it using your refund vs paying with a credit card immediately, but that is minor. I will keep using it every year and highly recommend it to anyone with simple or complicated taxes and just wants an easy process from start to finish.

I purchased Quicken Deluxe 2015 for Windows from Amazon. I run Windows 7. I upgraded from Quicken Deluxe 2014. I stuck with the Deluxe version because I do not use mobile options; neither do I use any of the advanced investment tracking features. At one time I was a Premier user, but found it labor intensive to maintain and not very helpful. I have other investment services that provide an x-ray function, so I did not think that this added feature was worth the effort, cost, or risk increasing the features I currently use.

My install of Quicken Deluxe 2015 went well. All of my Quicken 2014 account data was converted successfully. Registers seem to be fine. My monthly reminders are still intact. My existing password vault still works fine (I do not use the Quicken password). Budget data looks fine. Debt pay down still intact. Investment data seems OK. A reconciliation of one account actually corrected an account balance problem that I was having with Quicken 2014. One Step Update worked. So far, so good.

My usage background & history:
I believe I am one of the original Quicken users and have stuck with the product through the years. I stay with Quicken because it best suits my needs. During this time, I have had some significant problems in Quicken upgrades. Since I try to upgrade every year, I have learned to delay the upgrades until the initial onslaught of errors are found and hopefully corrected. Obviously, some upgrades have been extremely painful, others easy and error free. When I read the horror stories on the review sites, I always try to consider what version the user is upgrading from, the features they have had issues with, and the hardware they use before I make the purchase.

Way back, I specifically chose my bank because it had Quicken compatible checking accounts. Use of Quicken is the centerpiece of my financial management and I needed a bank that supported that. I appreciate all of the online bill-pay services and access to financial institution data that Quicken gives me. There are only a few places that Quicken does not connect to, but even then, they sometimes have the transaction download format that loads into Quicken.

My hope is that Intuit will provide as much loyalty to its customers as we have towards their products.
Turbo tax works.  Never been audited since using it.  Use it every year now as its cheaper than a tax person and easier than doing it the old fashion way.

Normally works great for me, but this year the state part had a glitch. So I reloaded , and still had to override  the tax table figures, I played around with it all day!!, so that meant it wouldn't E-FILE, so I had to figure what, and how to print, drive to town, and mail (certified), followed the tracking at USPS.com to find out what should have been overnight delivery took 6 days. Miss delivered to wrong post office sub-station where it laid for 4 days. Now my refund that I would have gotten in a few days will probably take weeks waiting for a check in the mail. The federal part was fine. I quit using Turbo-Tax due to big problems with their software 2 years in a row. Maybe next year will be better. Just remember the computer is your friend, and it's only as smart as the humans who input what it knows. TRUST,but VERIFY everything.

Over a 30 year user of WP in the legal field.  If there is so something you can't get the program to do, read the book.  I can't think of one thing it won't do.  Had to reorder program because of computer crash and over the years I just documentation.  This is great price.  Slight delay in arriving but on the end of USPS and not the company.
Great App for tracking progress. It is a great addition to the Kindle Fire. My son added for his personal use.

I have used Turbo tax for years and during that time I have also tried other products H+R, Jackson, blahblahblah. I always go back to Turbotax, if you are not sure of anything it will walk you through, if you are comfortable with the software you can use the form straight from the software. In the nutshell, this will get your taxes done quickly with the right forms and get you all set for your taxes. Those of you who like to be "creative" with your taxes this will also tell you how much BS your filing and your chances of being audited. The one thing I have noticed is that not all software can handle the states very well, people have complained about their state taxes being hosed somewhat.

Overall I give this a 5 star considering I could take my personal papers to a CPA, tax preparer, or try to hammer it out myself. It cuts the time down to nothing, 2 hours and I'm done.

Just remember to update Turbo Tax after you install.

I've had very good luck with Norton since way back in the DOS days. My System Works, works very well, but like most programs I don't let them run my machine, I run them. I'm no computer geek, but my PC isn't a toaster and I have to use my head. Norton utilities is usually one of the first things I install on a new machine. But I don't have any part of System Works running in the background, with the exception of the anti-virus. I use what I want, when I want and then turn it off. Over the years, I find this works best. The speed-disk utility and the Windoctor utility are the ones I use the most. They are worth the usual discount price by themselves.
Norton isn't perfect, and the documentation must be followed (turn off all that background junk that the machine has running) carefully, but like any good tool, once I've learned to use it, it works consistently, reliably and without glitches. In well over a decade and a half of using Norton utilities of one flavor or another, I've never had occasion to call customer support, so I can't address that issue.

Corel's Video Studio Ultimate x8 is a fairly easy to use video editing suite giving you all the tools that you need to create your own masterpieces.

It comes on 2 DVDs, the first is the program itself, or more correctly suite of programs as there are three, and the second DVD gives you a whole bunch of plugins and add-ons to the suite.

Installation is fairly painless, just pop in the DVD, select whether you want 32 or 64 bit, enter your key and relax for about 20 minutes whilst it installs.

The three programs that you get in the suite are the main video editing program, a neat little screen capture tool that is customizable and a program called Fastflick which makes short videos out of your pictures. Just select a template, import some photographs and let it do it's magic.

The main Video Studio program is basically broken down into 3 panels (which you can re-arrange, re-size etc. to suit your own style). By default the top left panel shows you your video, the top right panel houses your library of images, music, effects, transitions etc. and the bottom half is your editing timeline.

Importing a video is as easy as dragging it and dropping it onto the timeline. Same goes for music, transitions, fades, wipes and anything else you wish to do. You can split your video up, crop it, set key frames for transitions or to put it in slow motion, speed it up, color correct it at certain points if its over or under exposed etc. Basically you can do fairly much whatever you wish.

The range of options that are available to you may be a little bit daunting when you first start using the program. The best idea is to sit and plan out what you want your video to look like before opening the program. What background music (if any) you want to use, fade in or have a banner screen with some effects etc. etc. Once you have an idea, then open your program and stick to your idea. Once you are finished, if your idea didn't quite pan out the way you thought it would, then it's easy enough to change but at least your videos will look a little more professional than if you just start playing around with the options without any forethought.

Once you are finished editing, you can save your video out to disk (in a variety of different formats, straight to the web (options are Youtube, Facebook, Flickr and Vimeo), ready for a device such as your iPad or phone, or ready to record straight onto DVD. There is also another option for saving as a 3D movie (since I don't have a 3D TV I haven't tried this option).

One of the things I didn't like about Video Studio is the codecs that it supports for importing in the first place. Although it supports a wide array of formats there are some like DivX and some older AVI files that it refused to let me import.

It also takes a little getting used to controls (especially when editing text for title screens etc). But once you have it figured out then its easy to remember what you had to do for next time. One nice thing that you get included in with the suite however is a 45 day, all access pass to studio backlot which is an online training site giving you tips, tricks and how to make the most of Video Studio.

I'm the person that every year gathered my paperwork together booked an appointment at HR Block, watched as someone typed the figures into a computer and asked a couple of basic questions like do you have any other deductions (How did I know what constitutes a deduction, I'm not a CPA), paid my $300+ and waited on my refund.

After doing this for a number of years I realized that I could probably do this myself, asked around, was recommended Turbo Tax and haven't looked back since.

As I said, I'm not a CPA or have a degree in accounting. I don't know much about tax returns but that's the beauty of Turbo Tax, you don't need to. Just answer the questions that it presents and it will do all the hard work for you. When entering your W2 information, it tells you precisely what box it wants the number from and everything else is in plain, easy to understand English, with lots of help and deeper information available at a click of the mouse should you need it.

I've always went for the deluxe edition as this asks a whole bunch of questions about deductions trying to maximize your return. I've heard that there is over $1 billion each year in unclaimed tax money and Turbo Tax does an excellent job of asking you simple enough questions to try and give you back as much money as you can.

The year I started using Turbo Tax, I received a larger tax rebate than the years I'd been going to HR Block and yet my circumstances, pay etc. hadn't changed.

This years Turbo Tax is no different. I don't have my W2 yet so can't fully file so as a quick test I entered all of last years information (if you have previously used Turbo tax, it will ask you if you want to pull in last years information and have a lot of the basic questions like employer, names, addresses, social numbers etc. already pre-filled based on what you entered the previous year, saving you a bunch of time and typing). There are some additional questions and deductions this year and of course the all important Obamacare, which if you don't have insurance, the penalty this year has doubled.

Filing is as easy as a few mouse and you get the opportunity (at a cost) to retain a CPA should you get audited and want one on hand.

Turbo Tax has saved me hundreds of dollars of the years since I started using and I would highly recommend it to everyone.
Cool app for tracking the moon's phases.

I have been a long time Quicken user and I really like the software for tracking my finances.  I'm glad for the 27 month option as I'm not really certain that I want to be buying a new version every year.  There are some improvements in this new version over the other versions.  I do like being able to have Quicken update the incoming bills.  I'm not a big fan of the new color scheme for the pie chart showing spending.  All in all I am pleased with the software.

I say dirty/clean, because the graphical user interface is great but it won't let you break out of the grid it sets up to align fields and text for precise (read "space saving") placement. I'm also a Filemaker Pro user, and I miss that feature in Bento. (I also miss scripting, but that for serious work not my use of it.) It's "dirty" in that you can add column and those fields in your view anytime you'd like to very fast. In some DB apps out there adding fields, is a bit of a pain.

Also, there are a ton of user built templates of great quality out there you can download for free. I downloaded an inventory tracking app and a few others and they are great.

But the only real detraction from this great little app is that you can't create relational databases like you can with virtually any other database product, thus the lack of a fifth star. For what it does, it does well though, and this product is very polished. It feels a bit like a glorified contact manager that's been broken out of that box and expanded to handle virtually any simple flat database needs.

If you're new to databases, don't need relational abilities or scripting abilities and like simplicity and sleekness, then this is the product for you, hands down.
I think it's great.  Best doppler tracking of anything I have tried yet on my Kindle.  You can even get down to street level if you want to.  Forcasts are accurate and information is up to date.

I have used TaxCut for the past 6 years and have found it to be very reliable.
It's ease of use ... it has never crashed on me over the years.
The big thing is the free audit representation.

I wish I had read the reviews on Amazon before purchasing this product. I found it to be absolutely useless on a handheld device. DeLorme claim that it can be used on a Windows Mobile Pocket PC but fails to mention that you it doesn't work well as navigation device. You have to spend hours planning routes and entering waypoints in advance to your trip. Once that's done it cannot correct your route if you decide to take a detour or if there are road closures.

To use the navigation feature on you device you must do what called 'Tracking' to a waypoint, POI or following your predefined route. But for the first several hours of attempting to use it the program would automatically shut down when hit the Start Tracking button. Once it started working it was intermittent, but that didn't matter. When navigating, the map doesn't show enough detail, no matter what zoom level, to see exactly which streets to turn on and the voice directions rarely say anything, & when it does it's volume is low and doesn't even provide useful information. You must know the street names from memory before you get to them because the device wont tell you by text or voice.

If you want Aerial or Satellite imagery you can purchase it from within the software for a $50 minimum purchase, but can't seem to figure out how to add my own.

Adding new streets is simple enough but I have trouble correcting wrong street names and incorrect POI's.

If you have an internet connection on your mobile device/smart phone you're better off with the free Google navigation software.

We installed this product at home as an addition to our son who has studied CAD in high school and we hoped this would be a great help at a more reasonable cost than AutoCAD. As avid and above average computer users and troubleshooting skills large capability products like this don't normally worry him.

Here is the review he put together in the viewpoint of a student/CAD user.

===Install===
The installation itself, was as others noted, a bit difficult. Errors would appear. I quickly did a search and also read other Amazon reviews and saw the simple solution to use the shortcut it installs in the program directory and to reboot before closing the final error that appeared. CorelCad launched great from there. The lack of documentation that comes with the install CD is disturbing as noted by others.

I definitely did not like the way there were so many sub folders it defaulted to and changing some of them was tedious. I would have preferred if it simply asked on installation to take their defaults or let me pick. We ran a test install on both WIndows and Mac to be fair since it ran on both. I ended up running this on a PC inside of VMWare Fusion on the Mac to be honest.

Now my machine is not slow by any means but this program seemed to take a long time to launch and do certain actions. I hope it is just because of how complex the software capabilities are but make sure you have a powerful machine (way above what they tell you on the box)

===Features and function===
I loved the capabilities this offered. Pretty much everything I could think of was there. Finding where in the menu or toolbars was a learning experience like any new software. It would be cool if there was a menu to turn it down to "light" mode and reduce the options for simple drawing and then an "advanced" mode. This would increase the speed in learning curve or let you jump all the way in right away.

I created numerous drawings with various levels of complexity. The 3D modeling is great and awesome at such a price for this software.

The ability to read AutoCAD files and even export them made it easy to work with classroom stuff I needed to get done at home. So bonus points for that ability for all students and those needing to see AutoCAD files.

===Summary===
A great buy and an even better pricing for anyone needing this type of drawing power at home. With the ability to run on PC or Mac it reduces more limitations. Just make sure you have sufficient hardware to run it. Be patient on launch but then it performed well. I did not have crashes like others mention in any testing. Also if you are unfamiliar with CAD software in general expect a few minutes to learn something new. Explore with test drawings. Create new stuff. You cant break it making drawings. If complexity is the complaint then more exploration is needed. It lack of documentation is the complaint I fully agree. They really need to add more demos and help files.

I give it 4 of five due to the issue with installation and the lack of good help files. The rest rocks and I am glad I got this to work more at home on my CAD

===Updates===
NOTE: The new CorelCad 2013 update has documentation on the website and much of it can be helpful as long as you are not worried about it referring to a new feature you may not have. There is also updates for free to the version that comes on the CD that should be downloaded.

Doing your Federal taxes with this program is really easy. The program indeed goes step-by-step, answers all questions, has great written documentation, etc. etc. Basically, I highly recommend H&R Block At Home for doing Federal taxes.

BUT...if you are a California Resident who plans to use this program to do your state taxes, you may want to turn away. The California State program that this software download is absolutely rubbish. I would describe the program as incomplete, that H&R didn't bother finishing it before release. While the program retains the same step-by-step approach, it literally asks you questions verbatim from the tax forms. When you encounter some tax lingo that you don't understand and want to get the "plain English" translation, there isn't one. There is a bar on the right-hand side of the program that has a "FAQ" header, but there are no FAQs at all! To put the experience of the California State program in simple words, it is really the same thing as going through the paper forms and instructions themselves without any help available. So California residents, avoid this program!

I don't know if the California program has always been like this from the old H&R TaxCut days. I moved recently from Virginia and I never had any issues with the TaxCut Virginia State program in past years.

Cons: Tough to install
I wish I had purchased a DVD version instead of a Flash Drive to install on a laptop. To get a laptop or notebooks to boot from a flash drive is quite difficult. It would have been much easier to do it from a DVD.
Secondly, there is no documentation accompanying the thumb drive as to how to install Windows 10 on the notebook or computer. In the past, all my computers and laptops had either Windows 7 or 8.1 installed and installation was practically automatic. That is not true with installing Windows on a computer or laptop for the first time or as in my case on a notebook with Win8 installed. The only documentation provided was "Step. 1, Insert USB into device port. 2. Power on computer and configure it to boot from the USB, and 3. If prompted, enter the Program Key."

I bought Windows 10 Pro but after installation, my computer told me that Windows 10 Home was installed. It puzzled me in part because during installation, I was never asked for a Product Key and I cannot remember ever installing any version of Windows (I go back to Window 2) that didn't ask for that nonsensically overly long product key. It looked like I was stuck with Home even though I bought Pro. However, i located instructions on the Internet on how to upgrade to Pro . Following the instructions which included entering the Product Key and I was off to the races. But it took forever to install Windows 10. Notebooks and laptops automatically shut down after a short period to conserve power and that interrupts the "Getting Ready to Install" routine and if that happens, the process starts all over again. So, if installing on a laptop or notebook, change the power settings so that the laptop or notebook stay on.

Eventually, everything worked but Microsoft makes it as difficult as possible. It would be much easier if buyers had an installation manual. Otherwise, I am a big fan of Windows 10 but why does Microsoft make buyers go through such a tough obstacle course to get it installed.

I bought this because on the promise that most of the purchase price would be rebated. Guess again. In 30 years of buying software, I have never seen such a complicated rebate scheme and I have seen many bad ones. But I played the game only to be denied the rebate because I supposedly had not activated the product which I had. Unfortunately, I had to destroy the packaging in order to send in the required documentation so I can't return the product because it didn;t work a damn.

As soon as I installed the right click on my mouse ceased to work. I tried to get technical support but was unable to. I tried to uninstall it and it won't uninstall. One star for this product is one star too many.

When I try installing Dragon Speaking 10 P it didn't complete installation. It gave me an error message, "Naturally Speaking isn't installed completely. Please try reinstalling." The details are "The file SAPIDLL.DLL did not register itself." I tried the Repair program on the Installation Disk but it gives me an error message "Error 1500. Another installation is in progress. You must complete that installation before continuing this one." If I try to uninstall DrSp10 I get the same message. If I try to reinstall the program, I get the same message. I had the same problem installing Dragon Speaking 9P and never resolved it. '

All efforts to contact Nuance technical support (just trying to dictate the last two words in WordPerfect caused DrSp to go to the internet) were unsuccessful. Trying to use their database provided no results. However, searching on the Internet I found a solution and the installation was finally achieved. Now the activation window pops up every time I load NaturallySpeaking.

I have been using Dragon NaturallySpeaking since the product first was on the market, and it is never achieved the results that it promised or even came close. However, I must say that version 10 is more accurate than previous versions.

One of the shortcomings with version 10 is that it did not come with a quick reference card which is very helpful. The Help is terribly inadequate and since the user has no documentation is difficult to use many of the new features. The tip of the day is a nice feature but it is hard to use. The user cannot go back and the user cannot use Dragon Naturally Speaking menus while the tip of the day is displayed. Be nice if you have a catalog of the tips of the day.

Ordinarily, documentation is on the installation disk in the form of a PDF file. But not for Version 10. In fact, when I tried to use Explorer to open the disk to see if it had files, it immediately began the installation program and that screwed up the already installed program.

I tried dictating my e-mail address but that did not happen. Correcting the result was futile. Apparently it is necessary to create a macro but there are no instructions for doing that.

Nuance has a history of troubled programs and this is another one of them. Regardless, the program has promise and there has been some progress in making the program more useable.

This the worst I have ever seen. It consists of four CDs ecah with a separate program and no documentation. No menus and no way to navigate through the programs which are not integrated. The programs are progressive in that each user must start at the most basic level and progress. For a kindergartener, this may be alright but a student in a higher grade would be bored to ddath. The games are much too slow that much time is wasted. The only good program in this is the Art program which is excellent. Moreover, the installation programs do not copy the files off the CDs onto the hard disk so that the correct CD must be inserted each time the child wants to use the program. I sent the one I purchased bact to the Learning Company for a refund.

I bought this for my 6 yr. old grandson who just completed kindergarten. However, his favorite activity in kindergarten was computer lab so I decided to give him the opportunity to use a computer at home. I purchased Reader Rabbit 1st Grade. My opinion about the program is mixed.

It's worst problem is that it is very difficult to navigate. There is no documentation supplied with the program and there is no top menu provided. It is difficult to control. I am considered a computer whiz with almost 50 yrs. experience and one of my degrees is a Masters in Computer Science. Still I had numerous problems navigating. Fortunately, my grandson who seems to have an instinct for computers and who is very persistent found out how to maneuver. He is great on trial and error.

The program is not a teaching device. It doesn't teach anything. The student must already know how to do the work before using the program but it is a great refresher program. My grandson fell in love with the program. He worked five hours straight until he completed the program and then he wanted to do it all over again. The problem was that the program would not let him start all over again. Because the program has a memory for the work that the student has completed, when the student completes the program, all the student can do is to see the ending show. I spent almost an hour working around it. Uninstalling and reinstalling the program did not work. I finally had to create a new user account for my grandson so that he could play.

Although my grandson just completed kindergarten he is actually ready for the Second grade so he had no problems doing the work in the program. The program itself is not all encompassing of what pupils may learn in the First Grade. What a pupil will learn in the First Grade depends on the school. Reader Rabbit went about half way. The math is not as advanced as the local First Grade would have it and digraphs are not as extensively used in Reader Rabbit as some children will find in school

One other annoyance is that when the program installs it does not copy the files from the CD to the hard drive so that each time it is used, the CD must be inserted into the CD-ROM drive, making it harder for a child to start the program.

Also, some of the segments are so long they become boring in repetition. And there is way too much story telling which keeps the user from getting to the meat quickly.

I've always been impressed with the comprehensiveness and ease of use of Turbo Tax.  As laws and rules change they issue updates to the product as needed so that it remains up to date for the tax year.  You can electronically file both federal and one state.  If you need to file in two states there's an extra charge.  You can have your returns compared to others in a similar tax bracket, get indications of your audit risk, and you can sign up for an Audit Defender program, or speak with a tax expert if you have a particular question.  Most years I can file on my own with the assistance of this program.

Let me start by saying that using tax software is the only way (other than using an tax accountant) to prepare your taxes with any hope of getting them done correctly. I simply can't imagine going back to the paper forms, reams of confusing IRS instructions, and questionable results. Turbo Tax is a venerable entrant in the tax preparation software - and presents Basic, Deluxe, and Premier editions for 2007.

I like the 'interview' format, transfer of data from previous returns, the clear flow of the inputs through income, deductions, etc. and helpful audio information and support documentation. It is truly a worthwhile investment and bound to save huge amounts of time compared to paper preparation.

What I don't like is the Turbo Tax artificial division of products into Basic, Deluxe, and Premier. I purchased deluxe given that I have a job, a house, and a few investments for retirement. Pretty run of the mill - yet the "box guidance" says I'm at least Deluxe material, if not Premier. So what difference does it make? I can't say for certain, but I practically stumbled onto the deduction for expenses associated with Investment Managment Fees worth about $120 on my refund. I'm assuming the Premier version may have guided me more accurately, but it seems poor - given I'm entering stock dividends and capital gains - that TurboTax wouldn't think to guide me down the path of this common expense. Might have been my issue - but management expenses are listed as 'not typical' for taxpayers on page with a list of other weird deductions. Shouldn't this be a red flag for TurboTax when the check the return at the end!?

I'd also like to add that I filed electronically - still astonishes me that I pay and extra $17 to file both federal and state returns given that it must save everyone time and money to do this. Despite the e-filing, my state return was 'rejected'. TurboTax requires you to go back into the tax program to retrieve the reason for rejection and to make any 'fixes' needed. Well, the description of the reason for the state return rejection was so cryptic as to be illegible. In the end, without decent guidance from TurboTax, I filed the paper forms with my state. As far as the e-file goes? I don't see any way to get that refunded, so that's $17 down the drain.

Bottom line for me, it's still an incredibly useful tool. I still recommend it or something like it - perhaps TaxCut software which I have also used in the past. But take it slow, read carefully, don't assume that if the software lists an expense as 'not typical' that you may not have a deduction.

As a homeschool parent, I love finding educational apps that the kids enjoy.  This one has great workbooks with letters sounds, tracking, upper and lower case and more.  Fun Montessori workbook for kids and it has different levels of difficulty.
I love finding free educational games.  Especially when they are engaging.  This one is great for shape reorganization and for fine motor skills (shape tracking).

I stumbled on this software several versions ago and could not believe it contained so many aspects I care about....special effects, tracking, compositing and much more under one roof.  I use After Effects and this program resembles that.  Honestly I use lots of different software partially to inspire new techniques of doing my work, partially because I insist on filling my productions with new perspectives beyond footage...and perhaps I am a software ho. I was suspicious of HitFilm partially because the name, at the time, seemed a little cheap but it turned out to be the better part of a dream...and it keeps getting better.  There is a learning curve for each thing it does, which means there is a a lot of learning curves but there are good tutorial videos available.  I don't give anything 5 stars so this one did get my highest rating.
Just what I needed for my older Apple laptop.  Took a bit longer to receive and no tracking info was available but well worth the wait.  Thank you.

Not too excited about this app but it wakes me up.  I regularly use the alarm function but thats about it.  The sleep tracking function works well but I seem to always forget to press the "Going to sleep" button.  There is the ability to manually log the times but I rarely do this.
We have used turbo tax for over 10 years. The best part is that it ports the informal from previous years over to save time. Never been audited and got max refund..

Although many have complained about the loss of features in this version of TurboTax, I personally did not lose any needed functionality. I like TurboTax overall because it offers complete flexibility in the choice of an interview format versus entering data directly into the relevant fields. As a veteran tax self-preparer, I prefer the latter, but occasionally find I need to use the former for new material. There are aspects of TurboTax that are frustrating. I particularly take offense with the nickel and dime approach to adding additional state forms and the like. It's interesting that in New York, where additional fees to file electronically are illegal, there is no added cost to e-file, whereas in New Jersey, there is a substantial added cost.

It's Deductible could be a lot more functional. The degree to which I have to settle for selecting low-cost items that don't really match the more expensive ones I actually donated is frustrating beyond belief, but using It's Deductible does lower the risk of being audited. It's time for a major overhaul of the program in that respect. Finally, although cut and paste finally do work, the built-in tables and worksheets do not come close to approaching the functionality of a standard spreadsheet. After all these years, why can't one still delete or insert a row? That's just absurd.

One major bright spot is that by buying TurboTax through Amazon, I get a 10% bonus on my refund by taking it as an Amazon gift card. That is so nice!

Quickbooks online plus is a fantastic product, however it can get a little pricey. For example, I've owned quickbooks pro for a number of years, and always upgrade after 2-3 years (the most recent version I've owned is quickbooks pro 2014). With quickbooks online, you have to pay a monthly (or yearly if you choose) subscription price, where as the desktop version is yours to own forever. The amazon price is good for a year only, but after that you'll be at the mercy of intuit's price However, if you believe you'll upgrade every year anyways then it might be worth it. Looking beyond pricing, here are the features I've found most useful in quickbooks online:

1. It lives on the cloud: It allows me to work in the office & at home, without lugging my laptop around (this was what I used to do when I had the desktop version). With quickbooks online, you can access your company file from any computer with an internet connection (and there is no software to install). This also means your data is backed up and safe every time.

2. Mobile App: Quickbooks online also has a mobile app that can be downloaded for your iPhone (not sure about android or windows). While I believe the iPhone app is still a little basic, it does help me save time. For example, if I'm in the field and I incur an expense (i.e. we buy our workers lunch) I can take a picture of the receipt, enter the amount, payee, select what kind of expense it was from my chart of accounts, assign the expense to a class (I make heavy use of the class tracking feature because it lets me track income for each of my rental properties separately). After I click done, the expense posts right away (which means I don't have to save the receipt and enter it when I'm in front of a computer.

3. I save time entering transactions: I have my checking & credit card accounts connected to it. Quickbooks makes matching transactions that you've entered to a bank or credit card charge a snap. For example, if I buy food using my credit card and enter the expense either through my phone or the computer, quick books will let you match that transaction to the charge on your credit card statement. This makes reconciliation go by much much faster.

Overall: If you own quickbooks pro desktop and are considering upgrading, I'd strongly suggest you use the thirty day trial intuit offers to setup a sample company and play around with it. A lot of the features & terms are the same as quickbooks desktop, however the layout is a little bit different and you do have to play with the settings to make it suit your custom needs.
If you've never owned quickbooks before, then I would recommend the online version. My review is for quickbooks online plus, but you're needs might be met by quickbooks online simple start or essentials which are a little cheaper. Intuit has a comparison chart so you can see which edition has the features you need.

I have used this version for a number of years.  It has many advantages and useful features.  Much of its complexity is due to the tax code and not the software. One thing that is annoying year and year is that when you are done or almost done, there are always one or two requests for paying more money. One for audit protection if you care to purchase it. OK, I get that. The other is there is always a necessary payment for some useful service; I forget but it may be for electronic state filing and is about $10. The logic of that one escapes me. I presume somewhere in the fine print that I miss each year is you get your state taxes done but unlike the federal taxes there is an up-charge for electronic filing; I find that annoying BUT worse than that there are two methods of paying the extra expense; I over the years have tried both methods, neither of which goes quickly ... each takes about a half hour. One is use a credit card and one is deduct the money from your refund; all sounds easy but either method with so much online security takes too much time ... Except that I don't want another option to pick from ... why not just raise the price by $10 or so and provide what was implicit in the services anticipated.

I'll give it a 5 star for now. I've been using Norton's Internet Security for years now after a long vacation for them from the old days of a really slow computer.
Now with Windows 10, and have been beta testing for them since it started, I could not wait to get Norton back on my machine.
I've been running stock up until two week ago when I saw this on sale here on Amazon.
Everything as planned without a hitch. Received my key after purchase, a quick download installer and within a few minutes I was scanning my computer.
Good or bad, verdict still out, Norton's found nothing back, just some tracking cookies because I clear all that at the end of the day.
Only thing I have noticed is it seems to slow down the internet some, nothing bad, just not instant. Then again, I have only one cell tower to work off of out here in the country and it's more than likely that's what it is causing the issue. If I find out different, I'll be sure to update my review.

A lot of troubleshooting required just to complete the installation, e.g., disabling anti-virus, rebooting, running as admin account -- and honestly I wasn't sure what actually got me thru in the end.  Intuit is hiring data scientists constantly, but I'd prefer they put more effort into the user experience aspect.  Assumed accuracy to be fine, since I haven't been audited so far -- but the same effect as I used the Block in the past, which was cheaper software.

Quicken has become an accepted part of my financial tracking and planning, but as with any personal finance software it requires discipline to enter enough financial detail to make the resulting analysis useful.

My advice is not to "upgrade" annually unless you actually need a new feature. Most of the recent annual updates have been aimed at mobile device functionality which is great if you want it; personally I don't want any more personal financial information accessible on my cellphone than is already there, so these features are not worth an upgrade for me.

A disappointment with the program has always been difficulties with installation and software update, especially after Microsoft changes to Windows- this year's version seems more stable so far in this regard than last year's "crash on update" behavior.

There is a big Lightroom vs Aperture debate. I am familiar with both but I started with Aperture and so its my go to.

Overall, if you are NOT a pro but need something that gives you pro results, Aperture is what you want. It's the most user friendly and automatically interacts with all your social media, plus giving you a wide palate of tools and adjustments to do almost unlimited things to your digital pictures.

Aperture has great user interface, "Faces and Places" features, tons of output options, and really good camera raw support. For Mac users who wanna step up from iPhoto, Aperture is a natural...plus, heh, it's kinda the only "pro-sumer" photo app that supports MacBooks with Retina displays and iCloud Photo Streams.

There isn;t the "a free trial download" version any more, but the $79.99 app from the App Store, lets you install Aperture on multiple computers....which is a bonus if you, like me, dance between your laptop in the field and your iMac at home and/or collaborate with a friend or colleague.

Again, the main difference, to me anyway, is that Aperture's interface flexibility from that of Lightroom (and really most other photo workflow apps) in that it's not modular....which as a skilled amateur trying to expand my skills photography and finishing is one less lesson I have to learn amongst the thousands there are in the DLSR and physical photography world.

That is to say that Lightroom task-related in its interface, while Aperture always uses the same interface...with drop downs and tabs; which means you can navigate among your pix while making adjustments as you go, without having to switch modes like with Lightroom (Including make dupes so you can do multiple things to the raws). Aperture also does an excellent job of setting up and interacting with keyboard shortcuts—something for people like me who work on a lot of photos at a time LOVE.

BOTTOM LINE
For under $80, Apple's Aperture gets you capable pro-level photo workflow and editing software for your iPhoto library.

Plus, if you're deep into the Apple ecosystem (and if you've been a MAC user for any time you are) and even semi-serious about digital photography, Aperture is your software of choice; especially if you just dropped 2-4Gs on a new Retina MacBook and want to work with digital photography on it (e.g. which is totally AWESOME)

Yes, there are a few editing tools that you won't be getting editing tools as capable those that come with Adobe Lightroom (e.g. like specific lens-based corrections for geometry, vignetting, corrections for lighting, chromatic aberration, and image noise, etc)....AND at $149 Lightroom costs nearly twice Aperture's $79.99, i...AND if you REALLY want to do Adobe stuff, there bridge feature in Aperture is really seamless and works great.
Good when it works but always seems to have some irritating problem.  Won't email a code..wants to send text messages to voice phones.  Has trouble tracking account from different devices.

First, I am not an aspiring videographer, semi-pro, or even enthusiast. I’m just a regular guy looking to put together an occasional video or two every now and then. Since I do not edit video on a regular basis, I need a program that is intuitive enough that I can hack my way through it without having to read manuals, watch training videos, or take classes. Also, I’m not really a sophisticated user, either. While cool, stuff like chroma-keying, annotated tracking, and multi-channel audio are not things I really use very often, or even at all. I’ve used Pinnacle Studio in the past and have tried other video editors along the way. I’ve always come back to Pinnacle because it was easy enough to pick –up without any training. The last version of Pinnacle Studio I used was version 14. For this review, I have not read any manual or done anything to receive any sort of training. I am using my past experience editing video (and I’ve done my fair share) as my only guide.

Pinnacle Studio (PS) Version 18 seems a lot slicker but at the same time, has lost some of its intuitiveness. The new PS does not have a film clip editing mode (looked like a roll of old 35mm film), it only seems to have storyboard mode or straight timeline mode. Ok, so I tried storyboard mode. I entered a few clips and wanted to add transitions between them. On the good side, Corel gives you over 1,500 transitions to choose from. On the bad side, finding the basic fade-out/fade-in and cross-fade transitions among 1,500+ transitions was not simple. It is highly recommended to rate your favorite transitions (1 to 5 stars) and other effects once you find them so that you can find them in the future by sorting your effects by their rating.

In the old PS version, you simply dragged the transition icon between two storyboard images and that’s it. Well, in PS 18, you have to go to the actual transition location in the timeline and drag the icon there or right-click a clip or “scene” and add it from a menu. In the old version, to see what your transition looked like, you just clicked on the transition icon that sat between your two storyboard images and the viewer would show a clip of just how the transition will look using your actual video. To do this in PS 18, you have to go to the timeline and place the cursor somewhere before the transition and then view. This is completely workable but hardly user friendly for the occasional videographer.

Also, editing individual clips is less intuitive. In the old PS, double click on a specific clip or “scene” and you can easily crop the clip down to the specific frame. Now perhaps PS 18 has a really simple way to do this but I suspect that I will have to watch some training videos to learn how.

On the good side, video training is available for free at Studio Backlot (http://www.studiobacklot.tv/training/). You just have to invest some time. Also on the good side, Pinnacle Studio 18 retains its wide variety of output options. Not only does it cover almost all of the formats you’ll need, but it gives you a wide variety of compression options if say, you need to output your clip as a certain format (e.g., MP4) but you want it to be less than 25 Mb so that it can be attached in an e-mail.

Will PS 18 become my new video editor? That’s hard to say. I’ll give it the old college try and actually watch some of the training videos, but if the ease-of-use turns out to be like all of the other editors out there, I just may revert back to version 14 when I have to get something done quickly.

I am a casual amateur user of media, mainly enhancing and sharing for my own use with family and friends- nothing terribly fancy. That's why I really like this Roxio Creator NXT 5, which is a suite of media manipulation software programs that really could (and do in the case of Corel AfterShot 3 for example) stand alone in usefulness. The user interface takes you to a simple and welcoming screen that provides clear links to seven primary features: Burn Data, Burn Disk Image, Copy Disk, Burn Audio CDs, Create DVDs, Edit Video, and Copy and Convert Video. Each of these options has subset menu item trees with clearly described links, that take you to the function you need: Import, Edit and Transfer, Create, Share, etc. There are also powerful photo and music/ audio enhancers/ editors that will allow you to edit and save in numerous common formats, and if you've tried the Corel AfterShot 3 program before, this will be old hat, as it seems to have the same functionality as the original- like a free program within this useful suite. It's great to be able to add a pic that you've edited directly to your video, for example. The NXT 5 also supports editing from two separate cameras, which I find to be a real plus if you want to combine different views of the same scene in your final product.

I also played around with the audio editing tool, and it's very intuitive, allowing for visual editing of two streams of audio files. It also has a few fun special effects amplifiers like alien voice, which my kids loved. If you would like a fairly comprehensive media suite, without needing professional-level features, this is a cost-saving program that takes care of most home needs for media editing and sharing, in my opinion. It's inexpensive considering the component parts, and is useful out of the box. It did take some amount of time to upload, as some reviewers have noted here, but I've had no glitches with the uploading or the program itself since installation.

I have used Quicken for the last ten years or more, since it first came out, and love it. It's easy to use and does everything I need it to do, by tracking my budget, keeping records of transactions, everything. I recommend it to everyone I talk to about a computer finance program.
This is the first time as a Mac user and getting Quicken for it was a must. The program works really well and converted my Windows file over to Mac so I could start where I left off on my old PC. Wow, what a relief that was, and so easy too.
Keep up the good work Quicken.

Needed a method to convert some old VHS and digital videos to DVD's for archival and future family use.  This product looked like the solution.  I wish.  What I found was that the software, at least, and potentially the hardware were not up to the job.  First, the software (with the latest 9.4.x update) is very poorly documented, and so one struggles to determine how to use the tools, and what thw workflow should be inside the three "Capture - Edit - Make Movie" steps that are provided.  Second, watching the movie as you edit in the preview window I saw a number of times where the video and audio were repeatedly out of sync by a matter of several seconds.  Would this show up on the completed movie?  I can't tell you because the rendering software required HOURS to render a 50 minute video.  I can't tell you how many but I know it would be around 4-5 hours, however in every case the software froze before completing.  In one case, it decided after hours of rendering that the video wouldn't fit the DVD-R media when it had told me the 50 minute length would fit the 80 minutes available on the DVD.  Technical help in the package is very weak.  I have a fairly decent Dell system with 628 MB of memory, and it runs other significantly strenuous software (Photoshop, ProShow Gold, etc.) with no problems.  I would have liked to at least gotten one DVD compiled and burned to view the output - that might have provided some motivation for sticking with it, but, I cannot recommend this package to anyone, beginner, intermediate or advanced user.

I love iClone 5 Pro. There are a lot of great features that come with this program, right out of the box. there is also a lot of content you can buy online to add to it. They really make it easy to get started in animation. However, it could take a while to get really good at using a lot of the program's features. Luckily enough you can get a highly detailed user manual online that covers a lot. There is no shortage of documentation. It's easy to get started and easy to navigate. It's just that some of the really good features might require a little more effort and some reading but when all else fails, read the manual.

I am a computer professional who has designed and used software for many years. As such, I can say with authority that this software is some of the most poorly tested software I've seen from a name company (Intuit) in years.

As many other people have done, I attempted to switch from MS Money to Quicken 2010. I had read some of the poor reviews of recent, prior Quicken products and hoped that Intuit had addressed some of the issues with the 2010 release. Unfortunately, they have not.

The product is very slow on my extremely fast PC (running Windows 7). It takes over 10 seconds for memorized accounts to show when entering transactions. Frequently, the application freezes with a "not responding" message that goes away after 20+ seconds.

Of most dissapointment is that it's very buggy when trying to enter information. Both on-line payees and bill payments allow me to enter information, but after pressing the "OK" button on the appropriate windows, Quicken 2010 sends me back to the entry window with no error message and does not enter the information. (That's a BIG problem.)

As other posts have indicated, the support is very bad. Intuit's Web site has "24x7" chat support, yet when trying to use it on multiple occassions, I get an error message saying "an agent is not available". I need to try phone support. Unfortunately, the hours are limited. As well, email support has a 48 turnaround, which is not conducive to having to pay bills on weekends.

I'd like to think several of my problems are because I imported data from MS Money. However, I've seen multiple negative posts that suggest it's not Money but it's poor software development at Quicken.

I strongly suggest you consider alternatives to the product and let me know what you find! ;)

Turbo tax is much cheaper than an accountant, but it will not keep records for you. It does ask the right questions and sends the IRS as many forms as you need it to without an extra per sheet of paper charge. The product hasn't changed in years except for keeping up with the confusing government regulations. So as a repeat user, I am comfortable with it's format. There is very little extra work to complete my state forms and all I have to do is fill in my boxes correctly and it will let me know if I have left anything out

As long as I am forced to pay taxes, I will probably use TurboTax.

I’ve used TurboTax for several years now and each year seems to find the software for intuitive and easier to navigate I also appreciate that the program always seems to double check each section of the return for accuracy - leaving me with confidence that I not only qualify for the highest refund possible but an honest return least subject to an IRS audit. Turbo Tax rocks!

I really like the user interface and ease of use of Pinnacle Studio. I figured since Avid now owned this product, that the flaws and instability that plagued this product from version 8 would have been fixed. It seems a bit better, but still not a final release product. It behaves more like a beta version.

Bottom line: it crashes. The most frustrating thing is that it crashes sometimes after hours and hours of rendering. That means if you have a project that takes 30 hours to render, after 25 or 29 hours you will have to start all over again.

For those of you (like me) that need a program to write HD DVD discs, you can use Pinnacle Studio. But you have to eliminate menus, special effects, and any complex rendering. I can burn to a dual-layer DVD in HD DVD format by only using simple transitions/fades. It is better than nothing, as there are very few HD DVD disc burning software in the market. Most are now only Blu-Ray.

If you don't need HD DVD burning capabilities, then I recommend that you look at Sony Vegas Studio Platinum Version 9. It is about 6 times faster and very stable. It will do standard 480i and 1080 Blu-Ray. I use it to make 480i videos for people that do not have an HD DVD or Blu-Ray player. A project that takes about 25 hours in Pinnacle Studio, takes about 4 hours in Sony Vegas Studio Platinum. There are several instructional videos on You Tube that you can use and Vegas Platinum has built-in tutorials. It isn't as intuitive as Pinnacle, but works much better and is worth the time to learn the workflow.

Bottom line: If you need a HD DVD burning app, then use Pinnacle Studio to make a very simple disc. Otherwise, stick with the more stable Sony Vegas Studio, learn the workflow, and keep your sanity.

Edit: In case you were wondering what my hardware specs are:

- Intel dual core 3GHz processor
- 3 GB Memory
- Windows XP Professional
- 500 GB SATA hard drive
- Dedicated to video editing only.

This is an absolutely fabulous piece of software. I've been making my own greeting cards since 1994. I mostly used Create-A-Card. I had both versions - the one from Hallmark & the one from American Greetings. Those are very easy to use and come with a lot of content. But I had quickly outgrown the cartoonish images. But I continued to use the software because it was very easy to put together a greeting card & I could usually import the images I obtained from other websites. I ended up buying this from HSN during a crafting or scrapbooking sale.

Many of the negative reviews of this application mention having to buy content. The program comes with a voucher to buy content ($10) from DaisyTrail.com - at least mine did. Plus, Daisytrail also offers some free digikits. But IMO, you're paying for this powerful software application, not content (like Create-A-Card & apps that are specifically greeting card programs.) But you are not tied to DaisyTrail for your content. There are many other sites where you can buy content and even get some free. If you buy on sale, some kits are a dollar or two, which is less than the price of a nice greeting card. (My current favorite place (other than DaisyTrail) is TheDigiChick.) Or, if you are very creative, you can generate your own content. I'd say comparing CraftArtist 2 Professional with dedicated greeting card programs (like Create-A-Card) is like comparing Photoshop with Gimp or MS Paint. If all you want to do is crop & rotate images, MS Paint will suffice & Photoshop would be overwhelming. So if you're looking for software with a lot of content included, this is not the app for you. (Which may be why they call it CraftArtist rather than Create-A-Card!)

OTOH, if you want the freedom & flexibility to get content from other websites or generate your own, you will LOVE this program! It is extremely powerful! The digikits from DaisyTrail integrate easily into the program with the click of a button, after you buy & download them. If you get content from other sites, there are video tutorials on Youtube (from DaisyTrail) that walk you through the process of integrating them into your own digikit. I've purchased a lot of content from other websites and that includes many "frames" from other websites. When I dropped one into CA2 and then dropped a photo onto the frame, the photo was treated as simply another layer, rather than aligned into the frame. But I found a Youtube video that quickly & easily walked me through fixing this & now I'm using the frames I've purchased from other websites.

This software also is good for digital scrapbooking, but I don't do that.

IMO, this is a brilliant program that takes making greeting cards to a whole new level!

This app is great for tracking and sharing your shopping lists. It is easy to use and handier than trying to keep a paper list.
As usual...tried and tested...no problems...intuitive and I LOVE the Audit Probability checker function at the end.

Cyberlink PowerDirector for a long time was the fastest consumer video editor around but the last couple of years competitors have gotten close in performance. With this new 11 Ultra version PowerDirector again moves ahead of the others. There are now definitely improved color correction and ripple editing options better than those some competitors may have. There is also picture in picture updated design for animating visual elements. Most importantly it remains fast, very user friendly and helps all levels of users add a professional touch to movies. And there is an added bonus of a content aware editing module to assist beginners and new users to zone in on the best pieces of footage. There are some cons here as well. Most importantly there is a software that warns the user when imported media doesn't match the project frame rate. Sadly it doesn't guide the user on how to correct the problem. Also one would expect PowerDirector to be a bit more advanced on the new NewBlueFX effects. Good but could be better. But the good does far outweight the poor here. There are a huge range of features, 3D support, tons of effects and content from animation titles, blu-ray and dvd authoring and subtitles just to name a few.

If you have another video editor that you like definitely compare the features. Compared to some software out there this is only a slight improvement but compared to many it is a huge advancement. PowerDirector is certainly showing why they are # 1. Highly recommend this product.

This a great, easy to use app for tracking your spending. It allows you to customize it to fit your needs: including allowing to carry over balances and track by week, month or year. The pie chart to see what categories you are spending on is extremely helpful.

I just bought another copy of the "older" N360 2013 for the new registation Key, but I've been running this newer "2014" version for a few months already. Norton has the nice feature of alway being able to upgrade to the latest version as long as your subcription is still current. I have a little over 2 months left on my current N360 subscription and saw the 2013 version available a nice price here on Amazon, so I bought another 3PC one user box. I have to wait untill my current subscription expires or a day or so untill it does to enter the new KEY, because if I did it now, I would lose the remainder of my current subscription.

OK, I've said enough that is not really review material, but should be useful to anybody using Norton.
Review-wise, I think Norton 360 is a great product and works well for me. The only issue I have with it is the stupid cloud based (these days) password "vault". I preferred the older, local password manager, which on one of my PC's, is the way I still have it set up. I made the mistake of logging onto Symantec one day with my user ID and password, and it kind of "Grabbed" my password manager and made it the cloud based vault thing, which sounded OK, but when I logged into it with my Laptop, which had fewer log-ins and passwords, it for some crazy reason synched the cloud data FROM my laptop. When I used my desktop next time, the Cloud password data got synched TO my desktop and I lost 3/4 of my log ins and passwords. Fortunately, I back up my passwords to a local hardrive file with a built in, but awkward to find utility in Norton to import or export, and I was able to get it all back.
Other than that quirk. I like the software a while lot. It runs smotthly, and does not cause my PC's to crash. That IS pretty bad, but I was able to recover.
This has been a fun tool for my husband and myself with other family members to keep us working out. It is easy to use  with tracking daily and averaging your weekly work out & steps.

I am not a professional photographer but I take a lot of pictures with a pretty decent camera and this is the best edit app I've found in two years of using my tablets or phones to edit and upload pics on the fly. I upload pictures I took at least 5 days a week to a personal blog, often shortly after taking them. The blog is how our far away family and friends get to see our boys grow up- so the images captured are important to be in good quality. Right now I most commonly use a GrayBean card reader to connect my Galaxy Tab 2 10 to a 32gb sd card from my camera. I then use pic say to crop, lighten, smooth, contrast, color retouch and etc... I still occassionally buy other photo apps to check out but I've been using pic say pro for nearly 2 years from the android market (first on the original galaxy tab 7). I keep coming back to it for features that just aren't all in any other app I've found yet or because the resulting quality is better. This app is the closest I've found to being able to do on a tablet or phone what can be done with photo editing software on a computer, including retaining good quality and size of images.

This is my third iteration with Corel, having used VideoStudio 6 and 7. I originally started doing video editing with Pinnacle Studio, having gone through 3 iterations (7,9, and DV). The interface is very similar, with a timeline showing various elements - video, sound, voiceover, titles, transitions, etc - with drag and drop convenience for adding, changing and modifying elements.

Some of the folks at work use Adobe Premiere, which, from what I've seen, is a lot harder to use. For the average home user or wedding video, this has more than enough power: Video: Transitions, speed control including slow motion, fading, video tracking, Audio: Sound fading, voiceover,

There's so much to like about the newest Corel VideoStudio Ultimate X8 that it's hard to know where to begin.

First, installation is easy and faster than with X7. It supports Mac-compatible devices including iPadstouch and iPhone. Also Android smartphones, everything for PCs, including cameras and camcorders that recorded with internal memory..pretty much whatever you have or can think of . You do need a Windows-compatible sound card, Windows-compatible DVD-ROM drive, and Windows-compatible DVD burner for DVD output* (Windows-compatible Blu-ray Disc burner for Blu-ray Disc and DVD output recommended).

Installation was done in under 15 minutes. The Plug-ins took longer, but still less than half an hour. You still can have as many as 21 video tracks and four audio tracks. It is easy to upload video to Facebook or YouTube,

If you're editing 3D and full HD video, Corel recommends an Intel Core i7 or AMD Phenom II X8 processor with Windows 7 or Windows 8 64-bit operating system and minimum 4 GB of RAM. Ultimate X8 can edit 4K video, but they recommend a 4th generation Intel Core i7 processor.

"New MOV (H.264 format) support expands VideoStudio's export options, which also include AVI, MPEG-2. QuickTime, WebM, Windows Media Format, HTML5 and more" more formats such as support for AVI, MPEG-2, AVC/H2.64, WMV, MPEG-4, MKV and MOV (H.264 format) and more. The "blue screen" NewBlue Background Generator can turn your video into a nice background. Triple Scoop Music gives music to use, including some full vocals. The plugins have over 150+ effects for transitions and credits

Remembering back when I started video editing on the clunky cumbersome Final Cut Express, it's incredible to me how many sophisticated things we can do now with software that costs less than $90. So many things that used to depend on fillming technique can now be fixed digitally--shaky camera, poor audio, even lousy lighting. There's no reason not to create a professional-looking video when you have all these options, including adding professional-type filters, special effects, and lots of titling options.

The interface is the easiest I've used and the versatility of the software allows you to do pretty much whatever you want; I'd guess most people will never use even half of all that's available, but the parts you will use will give you smoother, better results than the earlier versions (if they had these capabilities at all). Great price, too. Definitely recommended.

Having struggled for years with Mac's Final Cut Express, I am a big fan of Video Studio, having discovered VS6 last year. It is inexpensive and very versatile software that is, as these things go, unusually easy to use.
Video Studio X7 tweaks the old VS6 with some excellent improvements--faster rendering and more sophisticated ways of editing your video. It has a new "FastFlick" feature that is great for people who don't want to spend a lot of time editing--just a few steps and you can put a video together quickly and easily (and, for many people's needs "good enough" esoecially if the end goal is YouTube. This makes it fast and easy to do.).

One Warning: Be sure to delete your old VS software before installing VS7--it doesn't work as an upgrade and your computer may continue to default to VS6.

One Negative: I took away 1 star because although I like the product very, very much, I don't appreciate the lack of documentation. They have only a short little blurb in the box--no booklet--and, worst of all, no "Help" menu where you can search for topics. If you want to do that, you have to sign in to the Corell website--imo, very irritating and even when you do it, not that well organized. There is no book published for Video Studio 6 or 7 but I have used this one with VS6 It doesn't explain, of course, the new features of VS7, but will point you in the right direction with many of the basic questions that will come up.

This is exactly what I was looking for in a medication reminder app.  I really like that I can postpone doses and skip doses rather than have a ton of notifications going off reminding me to take a pill that I don't intend to take (like some programs I've tried).  This works well for me because I have one med that I take at least once daily and a second dose is "as needed." There's even an option to shut off reminders during sleep time for things that are taken after so many hours (like cold medicine taken every 4 hours, etc). The only thing that could make this app better is the addition of a tracking log.  I would love to see how consistent (or inconsistent) I am about taking my meds and if there are any links between general symptoms I have.

I purchased this and while waiting, decided to read through some reviews. So here are my thoughts on what others have said.

What happens after a re-install?

I re-installed 3 times. Not Microsofts fault. Long story short - I have an SSD drive and kept trying different ways to force my new applications to install to my non-SSD drive by default and I did it wrong and rather than messing with backtracking to fix it, I just re-installed. No problems at all with authenticating and re-authenticating (but obviously, no changes in hardware).

Even if I did change hardware - I'd have to call Microsoft up to update them - I had to do this with Vista upgrade edition so it didn't strike me as unusual. This is a non-issue unless you want to take this OS to other PC's for whatever reason, and even then I'm sure a phone call would resolve it (I remember I had several purchased copies of Vista Upgrade and always forgot which PC each was installed on and it wasn't a big deal to call and have it activated, even on the wrong PC).

About the install: It's a system builder version, so don't expect it to install any rudimentary drivers. It takes about 20 minutes to install the OS and you are on your own for getting drivers (no network adapter installed, USB 3.0 drivers, etc). If you get your network card going then a Windows Update should get the rest but it takes a little more work than buying a retail version of the software. I'd say this added maybe 30-45 minutes to my install, not a biggie.

Otherwise, everything was as expected - it's Windows 7, no surprises that I can think of.

Update: All of the above still true - but I had a bonus I wasn't expecting. I lost the recover CD for my laptop. Toshiba wanted something like $50.00 to replace a recovery CD. I knew that I had a license key on my laptop (on the Genuine sticker) so I took a shot and used this CD on the Toshiba and entered the key on the sticker. Worked perfectly, just took a little bit of footwork to get some drivers after that. It's not a reason to buy this edition (probably works on other editions) but thought I would mention it to anyone who might be interested.
